---
title: "SAM: Systems Accounting Manager"
subtitle: "Architecture, Capabilities, and Deployment"
author: "CISL"
date: last-modified
---

# Overview

## What is SAM?

**SAM** is CISL's resource allocation and accounting system for NCAR HPC infrastructure.

## SAM
:::: {.columns}
::: {.column width="55%"}
**Manages:**

- User accounts and project memberships
- Allocation awards and balances
- Job charging and usage tracking
- Integration with XRAS (NSF allocation request system)
:::
::: {.column width="45%"}
**Serves:**

- Derecho (HPC)
- Casper (analysis cluster)
- Gust
- Campaign Store, Stratus (disk)
- HPSS (archive)
:::
::::

::: {.notes}
Brief intro — SAM is the system of record for who can run jobs, how many core-hours
they have, and what they've consumed.
:::

## Resources Managed

| Resource | Type | Facility |
|---|---|---|
| Derecho | HPC | NCAR / UNIV / WNA |
| Casper | DAV | NCAR / UNIV / WNA |
| Gust | HPC | NCAR |
| Campaign Store | DISK | NCAR |
| Stratus | DISK | UNIV / WNA |
| HPSS | ARCHIVE | NCAR |

---

# Architecture

## High-Level Architecture

```{mermaid}
graph TD
    XRAS([XRAS Allocation Portal]) -->|allocation awards| SAM[(SAM Database\n97 tables)]
    LDAP([LDAP / Active Directory]) -->|user sync| SAM
    HPC([HPC Schedulers\nPBS/Slurm]) -->|job records| SAM
    SAM --> API[REST API\nFlask]
    SAM --> CLI[CLI Tools\nsam-search / sam-admin]
    SAM --> WEB[Web Dashboard\nFlask + HTMX]
    API --> EXT([External Consumers])
    WEB --> STAFF([CISL Staff])
    CLI --> STAFF
```

## Technology Stack

:::: {.columns}
::: {.column width="50%"}
**Backend**

- Python 3.13
- SQLAlchemy 2.0 ORM
- MySQL / MariaDB
- Flask + HTMX
- Marshmallow schemas
:::
::: {.column width="50%"}
**Infrastructure**

- Docker Compose (dev)
- Kubernetes + Helm (production)
- pytest (~1,400 tests)
- pytest-xdist (parallel, ~65s)
:::
::::

---

# Database Schema

## Schema Overview

- **97 tables**, **91+ ORM models** (94% coverage)
- Organized into domain modules

```{mermaid}
graph LR
    subgraph Core
        U[users] --- UE[user_email]
        U --- O[organization]
        U --- I[institution]
    end
    subgraph Projects
        P[project] --- A[account]
        P --- AL[allocation]
    end
    subgraph Resources
        R[resources] --- RT[resource_type]
        R --- M[machine]
        R --- Q[queue]
    end
    A --- R
    A --- AL
    AL --- AL2[allocation\nparent/child]
```

## Key ORM Models

| Domain | Models |
|---|---|
| **Core** | `User`, `Organization`, `Institution` |
| **Projects** | `Project`, `Contract` |
| **Accounting** | `Account`, `Allocation`, `AllocationType` |
| **Resources** | `Resource`, `Machine`, `Queue`, `Facility` |
| **Activity** | `HPCActivity`, `DavActivity`, `DiskActivity`, `ArchiveActivity` |
| **Summaries** | `CompChargeSummary`, `DavChargeSummary`, `DiskChargeSummary` |
| **Security** | `Role`, `ApiCredentials` |
| **Integration** | `XrasAllocationView`, `XrasUserView` |

## Allocation Balance Calculation

```{mermaid}
sequenceDiagram
    participant C as Caller
    participant P as Project
    participant S as Summary Tables
    participant A as Adjustments

    C->>P: get_detailed_allocation_usage()
    P->>S: SUM(comp_charge_summary) by account_id + date range
    P->>S: SUM(dav_charge_summary)  by account_id + date range
    P->>A: SUM(charge_adjustment)   by account_id + date range
    S-->>P: comp_charges, dav_charges
    A-->>P: adjustments
    P-->>C: {allocated, used, remaining, percent_used}
```

---

# Key Capabilities

## ORM Patterns — `is_active`

Every model exposes a unified `is_active` hybrid property:

```python
# Works identically in Python and SQL
if allocation.is_active:
    print("active")

session.query(Allocation).filter(Allocation.is_active).all()
session.query(User).filter(~User.is_active).all()   # invert with ~
```

| Model type | `is_active` meaning |
|---|---|
| `ActiveFlagMixin` (Project, Facility) | `active == True` |
| `SoftDeleteMixin` (Account) | `deleted == False` |
| `DateRangeMixin` (AccountUser) | within start/end date |
| Custom (Machine, Queue) | commissioned / in date window |

## CLI: sam-search

```bash
# User lookup
sam-search user benkirk
sam-search user benkirk --list-projects --verbose

# Pattern search
sam-search user --search "ben%"
sam-search project --search "SCSG%"

# Project details
sam-search project SCSG0001 --list-users --verbose

# Allocation queries
sam-search allocations --resource Derecho --total-facilities --total-types
sam-search allocations --resource Derecho,Casper --allocation-type Small

# Upcoming / recent expirations
sam-search project --upcoming-expirations --list-users
sam-search project --recent-expirations
```

## CLI: sam-admin

Administrative commands extending `sam-search`:

```bash
# Validation
sam-admin user benkirk --validate
sam-admin project SCSG0001 --validate

# Reconciliation
sam-admin project SCSG0001 --reconcile

# All sam-search commands also available
sam-admin user benkirk --list-projects
```

## CLI Architecture

```{mermaid}
graph TD
    SS[sam-search] --> Core[CLI Core\nContext + Base Classes]
    SA[sam-admin]  --> Core
    Core --> UC[User Commands\n+ Display]
    Core --> PC[Project Commands\n+ Display]
    Core --> AC[Allocation Commands\n+ Display]
    UC & PC & AC --> ORM[SQLAlchemy ORM]
```

## REST API

```
GET /api/v1/users/                      → user list
GET /api/v1/users/<username>            → user detail + emails + projects
GET /api/v1/users/<username>/projects

GET /api/v1/projects/                   → project list
GET /api/v1/projects/<projcode>         → project detail
GET /api/v1/projects/<projcode>/members
GET /api/v1/projects/<projcode>/allocations   ← balance calculations
GET /api/v1/projects/<projcode>/charges
GET /api/v1/projects/expiring
GET /api/v1/projects/recently_expired

GET /api/v1/accounts/<id>/balance       ← real-time balance
```

## Web Dashboard

- **Flask + HTMX** — partial-page updates without a JS framework
- **Auth**: OIDC (production) / auto-login (dev)
- **Route protection**: `@require_project_access` / `@require_project_member_access`

```bash
# Run locally
docker compose up
# → http://localhost:5050
# DEV_AUTO_LOGIN_USER=benkirk (auth disabled in dev)
```

---

# Deployment

## Development Environment

```bash
# 1. Activate environment
source etc/config_env.sh

# 2. Start services
docker compose up                  # web app  → http://localhost:5050
docker compose --profile test up   # + test DB (port 3307)

# 3. Connect to DB directly
mysql -u root -h 127.0.0.1 -proot sam
```

## Production Deployment

```{mermaid}
graph LR
    subgraph Kubernetes
        I[Ingress] --> W[sam-webapp\nPod]
        W --> DB[(MariaDB\nPersistentVolume)]
        W --> OI[OIDC Provider]
        H[Helm Chart] -.->|manages| W
        H -.->|manages| DB
    end
    CISL([CISL Staff]) --> I
    EXT([External APIs]) --> I
```

```bash
# Deploy / upgrade
helm upgrade --install sam ./helm -f values-prod.yaml
```

## Testing

```bash
# Full test suite (~1,400 tests, ~65 seconds)
source etc/config_env.sh && pytest

# With coverage
pytest --cov=src --cov-report=html

# Specific areas
pytest tests/unit/test_query_functions.py -v
pytest tests/integration/test_schema_validation.py
pytest tests/api/ -v
```

**Test strategy:**
- Layer 1: Representative fixtures from live snapshot data
- Layer 2: Factories for write-path tests (exact counts/values)
- Schema drift detection — catches ORM↔DB mismatches automatically

---

# Summary

## What SAM Provides

:::: {.columns}
::: {.column width="50%"}
**For CISL Staff**

- CLI search and admin tools
- Web dashboard
- Real-time allocation balances
- Expiration tracking
:::
::: {.column width="50%"}
**For Systems / Automation**

- REST API (Flask)
- XRAS integration views
- Marshmallow schemas for safe serialization
- Kubernetes/Helm deployment
:::
::::

## Next Steps / Open Questions

- [ ] NCAR PPTX template — drop into `assets/ncar-template.pptx`
- [ ] Confirm audience (CISL-internal vs. broader NCAR)
- [ ] Add slides: XRAS integration detail
- [ ] Add slides: charging pipeline (collector → summary tables)
- [ ] Add slides: security / RBAC model
- [ ] ER diagram from live DB (`eralchemy2`)

::: {.notes}
Use this slide to drive the discussion about what depth is needed per section.
:::
